commits:
[{'sha': '6896ea0a4063be8555adff4163607a204011ffe3', 'message': "kubectl: strict check for exec command

Fix https://github.com/kubernetes/kubectl/issues/1745

The current implementation doesn't properly check for arguments between
the resource name and the dash separator. This can lead to unexpected
behavior when using commands like 'kubectl exec -it pod-0 bash -- run.sh',
which should raise an error but doesn't.

This fix ensures that when a dash separator (--) is used, there are no
extra arguments between the resource name and the dash.

- Only allow argsLenAtDash == 0 or == 1 (exactly one arg before --)
- Add test for extra args between pod and --
- Add test for flag-like args without dash separator"}]

commits: [{'sha': '03dd7fbe5be4e07b872b00c46e87ae341c937f09', 'message': 'kubelet: fix shared digest poisoning under NeverVerifyPreloadedImages'}]
review_comments:
["I am concerned that an attacker who can influence the CredentialMapping (e.g., by causing a pull of ANY image sharing the same digest under ANY other name) can make the kubelet treat a subsequently requested image as 'preloaded' even if it was never actually preloaded on the node. The fix assumes that 'if the digest was pulled under a different name, the current name must be preloaded', but this is not necessarily true. The image could simply not be present on the node at all under the requested name/tag. Specifically: if an attacker can arrange for image `attacker.io/evil:latest` (sharing digest D) to be pulled with credentials, and then requests `victim.io/sensitive:latest` (also digest D) with `imagePullPolicy: Never` and no credentials, the fix will set `imagePulledByKubelet = false` and the `NeverVerifyPreloadedImages` policy will allow the pod to start without credential verification, even if `victim.io/sensitive:latest` was never actually preloaded. The original bug is real of course, but the fix over-corrects by treating 'not in CredentialMapping' as equivalent to 'preloaded', when it should instead check whether the image is actually present on the node under the requested name.", "Thanks for the careful review @shachartal The scenario you describe can't occur because of the chain leading to MustAttemptImagePull. Before MustAttemptImagePull is ever called, EnsureImageExists calls imagePullPrecheck ([image_manager.go#L197](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/pkg/kubelet/images/image_manager.go#L197)), which queries the CRI via GetImageRef to check whether the image is actually present on the node under the requested name. If the CRI reports that victim.io/sensitive:latest is not present and the policy is Never, the pod is immediately rejected with ErrImageNeverPull at [line 130-132](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/pkg/kubelet/images/image_manager.go#L130-L132) and MustAttemptImagePull is never reached. Plus, MustAttemptImagePull itself guards against an empty imageRef at [line 185-186](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/pkg/kubelet/images/pullmanager/image_pull_manager.go#L185-L186), returning true immediately if the CRI didnt resolve the image (force pull). I tested this on a Kind cluster by buiding an image, tagged it as both attacker.io/evil:latest and victim.io/sensitive:latest (so they have identical digest), then loaded only attacker.io/evil:latest onto the node, then deployed a pod requesting victim.io/sensitive:latest with imagePullPolicy: Never. Containerd does not resolve victim.io/sensitive:latest by name even though the same digest, and kubelet immediately rejects the pod with ErrImageNeverPull.", '[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *karimzakzouk* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [sergeykanzhelev](https://github.com/sergeykanzhelev) for approval. For more information see [the Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=kubernetes%2Fkubernetes).

commits:
[{'sha': 'd983056393415c211e2e75ec74200846a30d3327', 'message': 'api: update validate.IfOption to accept non-pointers'}, {'sha': '1322ed178b9f3819187d971f3520a928b95b9c6d', 'message': 'code-generator: refactor validation-gen to use DeferredGen for cross-field accumulation

code-generator: simplify Context extraction and eliminate global state in validation-gen

Introduces a Context.Parent() helper method to eliminate the repetitive boilerplate that was modifying local copies of Context to derive the parent context. Applies this to discriminator, union, and zeroorone validators.

Replaces the package-level generatedPaths maps (e.g., generatedUnionPaths) used for deduplicating DeferredGen callbacks with a stateless approach: deleting the processed entry directly from the shared state map.

This ensures validation-gen does not leak state across independent invocations, making the codebase thread-safe and library-friendly without requiring a state reset.'}, {'sha': 'f3ea3ba818c9d85ea30a248836ae28b48bc866d2', 'message': 'code-generator: update validation-gen output tests'}, {'sha': 'd527d6c9a2046cece69c47f0b3889ab329223f31', 'message': 'api: update generated validation code for DeferredGen changes'}]

go
